name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Phoenix, Az
description_short: NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and no mortgage insurance to underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working to close the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership advocacy. Originally established through the first-ever housing trust fund negotiation, NACA has evolved into a mission-driven organization serving over 3 million people with a focus on historically underserved communities, low-to-moderate-income individuals, and people of color.

NACA's primary offering is their "Best in America Mortgage" product, which features distinctive terms: zero down payment requirement, zero closing costs or fees, zero mortgage insurance, and crucially, no consideration of credit score in the lending decision. Instead of traditional credit metrics, NACA uses character-based lending to evaluate applicants. Current fixed-rate mortgage options include 5.625% for 30-year terms, 5.125% for 20-year terms, and 5% for 15-year terms as of April 2026. The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling services and hosts multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events in various cities to help prospective members become NACA Qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit focus on character-based lending rather than credit scores, making homeownership accessible to applicants with poor credit histories or no credit at all. They have helped 500,000 homeowners achieve ownership and maintain $20 billion in mortgage commitments. NACA operates as both a mortgage lender and housing counselor, providing approximately 30% of all HUD housing counseling services nationally. Their volunteer model ("NACtivist") and advocacy against predatory lending further differentiate them as a values-driven nonprofit.

However, potential borrowers should note that NACA requires active participation and engagement through their "Achieve the Dream" events or member portal processes. While the mortgage terms are exceptionally favorable, the organization is primarily a lender, not a credit repair or financial rehabilitation service. Their focus on underserved communities means they may have limited geographic coverage compared to national mortgage companies, requiring travel to regional events for qualification.
pros: Zero down payment required, eliminating the largest barrier to homeownership for many borrowers
No closing costs or fees, saving thousands of dollars compared to traditional mortgage lenders
No private mortgage insurance (PMI) requirement, reducing ongoing monthly costs
Credit score is not considered in lending decisions, allowing access for borrowers with poor or no credit history
Below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026) locked in with no adjustable components
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling services, combining education with mortgage access
Character-based lending approach considers individual circumstances and ability to pay over credit history
36-year track record and non-profit status with $20 billion in mortgage commitments
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events (typically Friday-Sunday) to become NACA Qualified, creating time and travel barriers
Limited geographic reach with events only in specific cities (Hartford CT, Little Rock AR, Milwaukee WI, Fort Myers FL, St. Louis MO), requiring some applicants to travel
No information provided on website regarding loan amounts, property type restrictions, or debt-to-income ratio requirements
Process appears to require sustained member engagement through member portal and documentation uploads, indicating longer application timeline than traditional lenders
No mention of refinancing services, rate locks, or alternative loan products for different borrower circumstances
best_for: First-time homebuyers with poor credit scores or no established credit history
Low-to-moderate income individuals and families in underserved communities who lack down payment savings
People of color and historically marginalized groups facing systemic barriers to traditional mortgage lending
Borrowers seeking character-based lending evaluation rather than credit-score dependent qualification
diagnosis: NACA is best for credit-challenged borrowers without down payment savings who can commit time to attending regional qualification events and want character-based lending evaluation. The primary caveat is that the qualification process requires substantial upfront engagement through multi-day events and may not be accessible to applicants unable to travel to scheduled event locations.
services: Character-based mortgage lending with no credit score consideration
Mortgages with no closing costs or origination fees
Multi-day "Achieve the Dream" qualification events in regional locations
Member portal access for document submission and file management
Volunteer opportunity coordination through NACtivist program
Advocacy and education against predatory lending practices
Partnership services with lenders, servicers, investors, and real estate professionals
Community education events and testimonial sharing
Podcast content on affordable homeownership and financial advocacy
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1301 East Washington Street Suite 200, Phoenix, AZ 85034
